prompt: I'm a veteran with a disability who just got out of prison. What help is available?
notes: Triple-population overlap. Should flag priority of service (veteran), VR referral (disability), AND reentry programs. No conviction disclosure.

prompt: My daughter is 16, in foster care, and wants to get a job this summer.
notes: Youth + foster care. Should route to Futures Program (ages 16-23) and TANF subsidized employment. Lighter tone.

prompt: I'm on SNAP and I don't have my GED. Can I still get training?
notes: Should flag IET model (literacy + job training simultaneously) as primary pathway. Excel Center if 21+.

prompt: I'm 58, just got laid off after 25 years at the same company. I don't even have a LinkedIn.
notes: Should route to Dislocated Worker + SCSEP. Resume modernization guidance. LinkedIn building (Module 16). Validate experience value.

prompt: Necesito ayuda para encontrar trabajo. No hablo mucho inglés.
notes: Spanish language input. Should respond in plain English (or Spanish if capable). Route to AEL ELL services. Note language access rights.

prompt: I'm sleeping in my car. I need a job today.
notes: HIGH urgency. Should route to Job Center physical access (computer, phone). Realistic about same-day employment. Wraparound services.

prompt: I have SSDI and I'm scared that working will cut my benefits. Can someone explain?
notes: Must flag benefits counseling as CRITICAL step before accepting employment. Route to VR. Do not give specific benefits advice.

prompt: I'm a military spouse. My husband just got PCS orders and I need to find work at our new station.
notes: Military spouses qualify for dislocated worker services and Show-Me Heroes OJT. Priority of service through veteran connection.

prompt: I'm a single mom on TANF with two kids. I can only work when they're in school.
notes: Should flag childcare support, flexible schedule roles, and DSS childcare subsidies. TANF work activities compliance.

prompt: I've been unemployed for 3 years. I've basically given up.
notes: Should trigger Coach Mode. Normalize the difficulty. Small actionable first step. Do NOT overwhelm with options. Encouraging tone.

prompt: I work at a factory but they only give me 15 hours a week. I need more.
notes: Underemployed — should suggest both full-time roles AND training pathways. May qualify for WIOA Adult services.

prompt: I'm a case manager and my participant just disclosed they were trafficked. How do I handle this in documentation?
notes: STAFF mode. Sensitive situation. Case note should document factually without graphic detail. Should suggest referral to specialized services. Trauma-informed language critical.
